content.full_text:
April 24, 1990

Ms. Kathryn Donovan, General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

RE: Senator John Garamendi

Dear Ms. Donovan:

This office represents Senator John Garamendi. On behalf of the Senator we are requesting your advice pursuant to Government Code §83114(b) concerning the Senator's obligations under the Political Reform Act.

The Senator is a candidate for the Democratic nomination for State Insurance Commissioner. The primary election will be held June 5, 1990.

The Senator's controlled committee for insurance commissioner has received a contribution from a business entity with three general partners. The amount of the contribution is $1,000.00. The contribution check in question was signed, directed and controlled by one of the individual partners of the business entity. Each partner within the business entity has the ability to in fact direct and control political contributions made by the business entity.

The remaining two partners of the business entity did not participate in the decision to direct and control the contribution from the business entity to the Senator's campaign committee. However, they each desire to make personal contributions to the Senator's campaign committee this current fiscal year.

May the Senator accept contributions from the two remaining partners if those contributions are made from each respective partner's personal funds?

Page Two
Ms. Kathryn Donovan

Thank you for your attention in this matter. I look forward to your response at your earliest convenience.

Very truly yours,

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL, FONG & LEIDIGH

LANCE H. OLSON

cc: Senator John Garamendi
